Guard of the Marines  
 

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: SECONDARY
AC: 8
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
SV MAGIC: +10
WT: 10.0 Size: LARGE
Class: WAR CLR PAL RNG SHD BRD ROG SHM
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)
Guard of the Marines
Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: SECONDARY
AC: 8
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
SV MAGIC: +10
WT: 10.0 Size: LARGE
Class: WAR CLR PAL RNG SHD BRD ROG SHM
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Item Lore:Danak Armory Marine Shield
Item Type:Shield
Stackable:No
Submitted By:Cazziel Uln'Hyrr
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-10 17:59:04
Page Updated:Mon Jan 11th, 2021

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Rarity: Uncommon
Level to Attain: 35
[Comments ]

This item is the result of a quest.
Expansion List - Premium only.
Quest Name
Protect the Shipyard

Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
The Overthere
Item Lore: Danak Armory Marine Shield


Uploaded November 27th, 2008
Send a correction
Post Comment
Droped too
QuoteReply
# Nov 02 2004 at 2:19 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1633306622566
8 posts
This is also Droped by Watch Sergeant Grolj in OT. When the Glowing Golem is killed for the SK Greenmist quest; this guy is also spawned. Kill him get the shield.
▲ Page top
Must read !
QuoteReply
# Aug 27 2003 at 2:23 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592255098754
Scholar
39 posts
Charming the guy to do the jade turn in is pointless since you need to be Amiable. (Charming NPCs sets thier base faction at Indifferent).

Forget wasting time waiting around for people to hunt Kunark Dragons so you can hit them for faction. If you are determined and want your hammer with immediate results, keep reading.....

How you get your Overthere Hammer the easy way in 1 day of work no matter what race you are:

#1: Buy (from players) or have a Shaman make you an Essence of Darkelf potion (Illusion: Darkelf).

#2: Slay tons of Sarnak Berzerkers and bank the whips. (Use PoK book to bank them in PoK bank everytime you fill your backpacks).

#3: When you have gathered 32 STACKS of whips (thats 128 total whips) you can get near the outpost in OT and drink your potion to become a darkelf and have "Apprehensive" base faction as that race.

{Note: A stack of whips always means 4 whips at a time.}

#4: Then go into the outpost bank and get out your whips to turn in to Captain Rottgrime (hail him to stop him so he doesn't pace back and fourth between his Marines and cancel the trade).

Closing: While my Iksar Shadowknight mule was in Darkelf form I went from Apprehensive to Indifferent after 10 STACKS of whips. After 12 MORE STACKS of whips I went to Amiable. Then I bought one jade from one of the Gnome vendors in the Outpost and ran it to the Skeletal Foreman and turned it in for my Overthere Hammer.

{Note: When I ran out of the Outpost and targeted a Cliff Golem and canceled my Darkelf Illusion, I was "Scowled" at instead of "Amiable" in my natural Iksar form. Iksar have VERY bad faction with most NPCs in Everquest and I may need to turn in many hundreds more to be loved in my natural Iksar form.}

Too bad i'm not that insane to go through with the last part.

I hope this method helps you get your Overthere Hammer in one day. (It took me ALL DAY to do this.... literally.)

EDIT: YUCK Whats wrong with Sony? They broke faction and illusions big time. I tried to pull a trick with a new character and I was KOS and died. Disreguard my old old method that I posted above until Sony fixes that faction (if they ever return it back to the way it was when I made this guide.)

Edited, Fri Apr 30 02:13:02 2004
▲ Page top
RE: Must read !
QuoteReply
# Apr 01 2004 at 5:54 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592256029093
30 posts
Actually charming only makes the mob indifferent to everyone else. The person charming the mob sets the faction to amiable. I should know, my necro charmed the dude for the gate hammer just fine.
▲ Page top
RE: Must read !
QuoteReply
# Feb 26 2004 at 8:58 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1675037946721
67 posts
First off, you are talking about the hammer that procs the OT gate, which has NOTHING to do with this item, keep these things to thier own posts. Secondly, you're wrong in your assessment of charming. I have a human bard who worships bristlebane, I simply drank the potion, charmed him, hailed him, followed the text line, gave him the jade and poof, I have the hammer, I haven't turned in any whips, braids or anything else and I have done a lot of killing in OT. Took me about 15 mins to finish.
▲ Page top
RE: Must read !
QuoteReply
# Jul 03 2004 at 6:57 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592257152009
15 posts
Quote:
I have a human bard who worships bristlebane, I simply drank the potion, charmed him, hailed him, followed the text line, gave him the jade and poof


by charming him you are automatically amiable, however if you charm him, and your friend cons him, he will only be indifferent.

by charming him you are automatically amiable, however if you charm him, and your friend cons him, he will only be indifferent.

Braids
QuoteReply
# Sep 09 2002 at 1:11 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591767656072
10 posts
You have to turn in a ton of braids to see your faction increase. Don't expect that turning in a few you will instantly be allied with VS faction. I had to turn in over 100 braids b4 I could do the Overthere hammer quest i.e. get to amiable.

Edited, Mon Sep 9 01:58:40 2002
▲ Page top
Faction !
QuoteReply
# Aug 28 2001 at 9:30 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592758051688
View User Forum
8 posts
IF YOU GIT RIDE Of THE SHELD YOU LOOSE ALL THE WORK YOU JUST DID!

IT WILLL RESETS YOUR FACTION!

just so you know,so you dont wonder,

Why in the world is it taking me so Long to Git my Faction UP :)


7th hammer Rocks
▲ Page top
RE: Faction !
QuoteReply
# Aug 15 2002 at 9:34 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591774623301
63 posts
BZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZTTT!!!! WRONG!!

The quest itself, not the quest reward, is what raises your faction. Did you see the message on your screen that read "Your faction standing with Venril Sathir got better"? That has nothing to do with the fact that you also just got a shield, nor does it have anything to do with what happens to said shield. The only way to undo your work is to do something that causes the message "Your faction standing with Venril Sathir got worse" to appear on your screen. Namely, hunting TD Ogres, KC, or (at high levels) Goons.
▲ Page top
Location of Berserkers?
QuoteReply
# Jun 05 2001 at 4:16 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591759195525
1 post
I spent an hour killing the eastern Sarnak Camp in OT... I got 8 war braids only. My faction didn't even go significantly.. Is there a place overrun with Berserkers? Raping sarnaks for 8 hours a day, 7 days a week for a month isn't my idea of fun.
▲ Page top
RE: Location of Berserkers?
QuoteReply
# Feb 16 2004 at 2:35 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591800738671
3 posts
You shouldn't waste your time at the ruin camps... they mostly spawn knights and casters. Berks only pop maybe 1 in 20. but they are a random mob wandering the zone. Most often you will see them wandering near the southern watering hole (not the lowbie mudpit... the one just east of skyfire zone) they hang out around that area and are really common I'd say about 1 berk to every 2 or 3 knights. the only problem with that is groups. there are usually a bunch of grouping goods who have no care (or knowledge) of the no drop braids when they kill the berks so they loot and destroy. if there is a group ask someone in the group if they will part with the braids.
▲ Page top
binding
QuoteReply
# May 27 2001 at 5:48 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Actually you can bind in the overthere.. just go to the outpost and swim around it to the beach. Be careful no to get to close to the dock if you are kos but in the corner along the wall is a bind point. I have been bound there by many a shaman.
▲ Page top
Reason For Quest
QuoteReply
# May 24 2001 at 3:34 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
The reason for the Quest is to get your faction up so that you can do the hammer quest. After faction is Amiable you talk to the foreman and he says give me two jades and he gives you a giant hammer so have a sewing kit or open slot and it is 6 50 2hb, PROC is port to OT at LvL 50. That is the reason behind the braids, Also i tried giving the dumb shield to my pet and it doesnt show up so i assume for some dumb reason he just cant use it
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 22 2003 at 1:17 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) you cant give a nodrop item to a pet....its "no drop"...
Complaints
QuoteReply
# May 20 2001 at 9:27 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591756045091
13 posts
You know, I listen to players of good races complaining that 'I can't bind in Overther, I can't bind anywhere near Lavastorm.' Well, deal with it! As a Dark Elf Shadowknight of Innoruuk (and a roleplaying one at that) their are a very few places I can bind. So when I see the good races that have over half the world to bind at, I look at them ad think, learn to live with the fact that it's a short distance to bind in Firiona Vie!

RE: Complaints

# Oct 28 2002 at 2:40 PM Rating: Excellent

4 posts
Now that PoP is out, good races can bind in the Plane of Knowledge and just port down to OT. No problem at all.

RE: Crap!
QuoteReply
# Apr 23 2001 at 8:03 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
It does look better than the SBS. Once you have amiable faction with the Outpost, you can hand a jade to the skeleton foreman & get a Worker's Sledgemallet. This hammer procs (at lv50) a Gate to the Overthere (you appear near the ruined boat the skeletons are working on)
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Mar 22 2001 at 2:11 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) im a necro so i sell it as a way to make some sstuff cheaper and a shield for my skelly
RE: Crap!
QuoteReply
# Mar 29 2001 at 11:38 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
It's no-drop so how do you sell it?

The main benefit of this quest is that it's one of the only ways to increase faction in the OT outpost.
It's ridiculously easy to aquire the Warbraids, every Sarnak Berserker is carrying them.

So for all you people who are tired of getting molested by the Dragoons, you might consider a way to give those warbraids to Rottgrime
▲ Page top
RE: Crap!
QuoteReply
# May 14 2001 at 7:24 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Ya, but most of the peeps that are KoS to the dragoons and whatnot are also KoS to most of the junk in OT, it would be nice to be able to work on faction there. Its a nice place to bind, but besides that, theres really not all too much for good guys. ;(

Nem Tsunami
48 War
The Rathe
▲ Page top
RE: Crap!
QuoteReply
# Jun 14 2001 at 5:05 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593965790794
328 posts
Question for you guys....

As a good race, would it not be possible to have a high level enchanter, charm Rott, and then you give him the braids? Or does the charming negate the quest?
▲ Page top
RE: Crap!
QuoteReply
# Nov 08 2002 at 3:50 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591773994568
Guru
658 posts
There are no faction hits when doing a quest with a charmed NPC. Of course, if you're an enchanter you can charm the guy you get the hammer from and get the hammer anyway, without doing this quest.
▲ Page top
RE: Crap!
QuoteReply
# Jul 13 2001 at 3:57 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361490149
6 posts
You'd also be much better off waiting another 20 levels or so and going on a Faydedar hunt, or any of the other Kunark dragons. Killing the Kunark dragons is the only other way to get OT faction (Venril Sathir) up, other than the braid quests that is.
▲ Page top
RE: Crap!
